patterns_recognized: HARD CAP 7's sub-0.02 hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap band has produced 3 consecutive false negatives (7/10 +0.018, 7/12 +0.0189, 7/18 +0.0185, all winners); TB@BOS (+0.0117) and MIA@MIL (+0.0185) both fall in that band today. Not overriding either — this is a documented pipeline-calibration issue already flagged for a future threshold fix, not something a per-game executive read should re-derive absent live news.

D-grade dual-opposing-signal picks (SP-xERA against + PQI against) remain a stake-suppression signal only, never a directional fade — applied strictly across TEX@ATL, PIT@CLE, SD@KC, MIN@CHC, DET@LAA, SF@SEA, and STL@ARI today, even where opposition is extreme (DET@LAA's 5-dimension stack mirrors yesterday's correctly-predicted DET shutout).

BAL@HOU's standing 7/17-7/18 caution explicitly requires the SAME opposing SP-xERA+PQI shape as those two BAL wins; today's signals point the other way (both confirm HOU), so the caution does not extend to this game — confirmed at GOLD rather than reflexively downgraded on series momentum alone.

CIN@COL, WSH@OAK, and SF@SEA are each running matchup-specific extreme variance (lopsided results in both directions on consecutive days); treating recent same-pair scorelines as non-predictive and reading only today's signal configuration for each, per the explicit 7/18 postgame warnings on all three pairs.

F5-only signals without F1/F3 corroboration remain an insufficient GOLD anchor — LAD@NYY's F5_bullpen_gap=+0.98 is the identical unsupported signal that correctly triggered a DOWNGRADE on 7/17 (NYY lost 2-1) and recurred through the 7/18 postponement.

CHW @ TOR.reasoning: Pre-cap score 2 (PQI +6.2 confirms, Stage agree) with no F-signal or SP-xERA anchor; HARD CAP 10 held pick_prob=0.5001 at zero absent a qualifying rescue. Same TOR-over-CHW pairing produced a 1-0 TOR win on 7/18 under an identical thin two-dimension stack that was correctly SKIPped. Statsapi confirms Yesavage/Burke as scheduled, no scratches. No basis to elevate a stack this thin.

LAD @ NYY (G1).reasoning: F5_bullpen_gap=+0.98 is the only firing signal — no F1 or F3 corroboration, no SP-xERA or PQI support in the grade stack. This is the identical thin profile that correctly triggered a DOWNGRADE on 7/17 (NYY lost 2-1) and carried into the 7/18 postponement notes verbatim. Standing rule: F5-only without F1/F3 is insufficient for GOLD conviction. Schlittler/Yamamoto confirmed via statsapi, no lineup surprise. DOWNGRADE to SKIP.

TB @ BOS.reasoning: Pre-cap A- (SP 0.38xERA agrees, PQI +8.3 confirms, Stage agree) is suppressed only by HARD CAP 7 on hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0117 — inside the documented sub-0.02 false-negative band (three straight misses 7/10-7/18). That pattern is already flagged in the archive for a future pipeline threshold revision; without live news of my own on this game, re-deriving the cap's math isn't my role today. CONFIRM the cap as-is.

NYM @ PHI.reasoning: Score 2 stack (PQI -5.5 confirms, Stage agree) with no F-signal or SP-xERA anchor — below the GOLD bar. Rangel/McLean confirmed as scheduled via statsapi. Nothing in the recent archive flags this specific pairing; an ordinary SKIP with no elevation case.

Per the standing 7/11 archive rule, D-grade dual-opposing signals are stake-suppression only — do not fade to the opposing side, directional splits in this configuration run close to 50/50. PIT @ CLE.reasoning: D-grade dual-opposition: Paul Skenes gives PIT a real 1.17xERA edge and PQI runs -12.7 against CLE. Skenes' quality edge is genuine, but per the archive's D-grade rule this is a suppression signal, not a fade mandate. CONFIRM SKIP, no override.

The 7/17-7/18 BAL-over-HOU caution required the SAME opposing SP-xERA+PQI shape as those two losses; today both signals point toward HOU instead, so that specific caution does not extend here. bullpen_fatigue/short_term_skill stress flags are noted but didn't trigger a cap. Hunter Brown confirmed via statsapi. CONFIRM GOLD.

SD @ KC.reasoning: D-grade: SP 0.73xERA against SD (Cameron) plus Stage 1/2 disagreement (Δ=0.1246, Stage 1 favored KC). hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=-0.0389 modestly favors SD but doesn't offset the SP/Stage read. Suppression-only per the standing D-grade rule — CONFIRM SKIP, no fade.

MIA @ MIL.reasoning: Triple suppression: HARD CAP 7 (hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0185, same sub-0.02 false-negative band as three prior misses), HARD CAP 10 bottom-bucket, and edge 3.8pp under the 4pp floor. Pre-cap stack (F3=3225.7, F5=+0.86, SP agrees) is directionally solid, and this exact profile also appeared on 7/18 (MIL won 8-6) — but that's a documented pipeline-calibration pattern, not live news I'm bringing to the table, so I'm not overriding the caps today. This exact pair split 1-1 the last two days when opposing signals were tested (7/17 MIN won against a CHC pick, 7/18 CHC won against a MIN pick) — the dual-opposing shape is stake-suppression only, not a directional lean either way. Coors variance remains a live caution worth tracking, but nothing here argues for downgrading a confirming stack. CONFIRM GOLD.

This is the same DET@LAA series as 7/18, where an equally extreme opposition profile correctly predicted a DET shutout. The standing rule against fading D-grade opposition still applies regardless of how strong the lean looks — CONFIRM SKIP, no OVERRIDE.

LAD @ NYY (G2).reasoning: PENDING_SP_DATA — statsapi shows no probable starter listed yet for either side of game 2. Pipeline correctly withholds a directional call pending the 100+ Statcast pitch minimum once starters are announced. Hold confirmed; fill in at next workflow run.
